description_long: This is a dark metallic kettle with a cylindrical body and a slightly flared, faceted base. The surface is adorned with intricate engraved patterns, including a prominent stylized bird with outstretched wings and Norse-inspired motifs, as well as circular Celtic knotwork. The kettle features a pronounced, arched side handle made of the same metal, attached at the top and bottom. The spout is integrated into the rim, forming a subtle, angled pour lip. No lid is visible, leaving the top open and exposing the interior, which appears to be made of the same metal as the exterior. The overall finish is matte with a slightly rough, aged texture, giving it an antique or historical appearance.
description: A dark, engraved metal kettle with a side handle, open top, and intricate Norse and Celtic patterns. The surface is matte and slightly rough, with a subtle spout and no visible lid.
description_view_0: Front view showing the engraved bird motif and Norse runes on the main body, with the base and rim clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the arched handle and circular Celtic knotwork, as well as the angled spout lip.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the handle attachment and the continuation of the engraved bird motif inside the rim.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displaying more Celtic knotwork and the open top, with the handle partially visible.
